rent.note: furnished 1BR in Kuching; RM 700-3,900 range; mid-range ~RM 1,500 ($335)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; Sarawak electricity slightly cheaper than Peninsular

groceries.note: Pasar Satok weekend market; local food very affordable; Sarawak laksa and kolo mee

diningOut.note: Kuching known for food scene; hawker prices very low; $1-3 meals common

transit.note: Grab + some need car; Kuching is spread out; lower traffic vs KL

coworking.source: malaysia4u.com cost guide / local coworking listings
coworking.note: limited coworking options in Kuching; cafes used as workspace; growing slowly

simData.note: Celcom/Unifi Mobile; Sarawak coverage good in city; RM 50-60/mo

misc.note: nature activities (Bako NP, Semenggoh orangutans), entertainment, personal care

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.claim: Kuching cost of living very low; couple can live on under $600/month home-owning
evidence.quote: The cost of living is very low, less than US$600 a month for a home-owning couple. Prices in Kuching are inexpensive, even when compared with other Malaysian destinations

evidence.claim: Kuching 30-40% cheaper than KL; furnished apartments RM 700-3,900/month
evidence.quote: smaller cities like Ipoh, Malacca, and Kuching can be 30-40% cheaper than KL for comparable lifestyles. Furnished apartments in Kuching range from as low as RM 700 per month for a 2-bedroom unit to RM 3,900 per month

evidence.claim: Kuching Renna Residence 2BR expat-friendly RM 3,000; 3BR Skyvilla RM 1,600
evidence.quote: RM 1,600 per month for a fully furnished 3-bedroom apartment at Skyvilla Residence in MJC, and RM 3,000 per month for a 2-bedroom unit at Renna Residence marked as Expat-Friendly

evidence.claim: Note: Kuching not covered by DE Rantau digital nomad visa (Peninsular Malaysia only)
evidence.quote: Kota Kinabalu and Kuching are not covered by Malaysia's DE Rantau digital nomad visa, which only applies to Peninsular Malaysia
